title: Nadaa

{{Quote|Are you one of Saquesh's men? Did you take my daughter?|Nadaa|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
'''Nadaa''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] who lived with her daughter, [[Adana (Human)|Adana]], in the [[Refugee Sector]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]], in [[3951 BBY]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], her daughter was kidnapped by the local [[Exchange]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] [[Saquesh]] as Nadaa owed the Exchange 600 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. While visiting the moon, the former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Meetra Surik]] spoke with Nadaa and agreed to help free her daughter. During her meeting with Saquesh, Surik was unsuccessful in her negotiations and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Saquesh to free Adana. Nadaa was extremely grateful afterward.

==Biography==
{{Quote|The Overseer… Saquesh… he took my daughter to sell to the Hutts. He took my daughter to sell to the Hutts. All because I told him I could not pay his tribute.|Nadaa|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
[[File:Adana1.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Adana, Nadaa's daughter]]
Near the end of the [[Dark Wars]] in [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="CAMP">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Nadaa was a [[Refugee/Legends|refugee]] who lived with her daughter, [[Adana (Human)|Adana]], in the [[Refugee Sector]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Adana was kidnapped and held hostage<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|127|Saquesh}}</ref> by the [[Exchange]] crime organization overseer of the area, a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> male<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] named [[Saquesh]], due to Nadaa's unpaid tribute of 600 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to the Exchange. Saquesh planned to sell Adana to the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutts]] to pay the debt.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

While visiting the moon [[Mission to Nar Shaddaa (First Jedi Purge)|searching]] for [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Zez-Kai Ell]], [[Meetra Surik]], a former [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], spoke with Nadaa. She told Surik the Exchange was holding her daughter and begged Surik to help her. Surik agreed to help Nadaa rescue Adana, and she went to meet with Saquesh.<ref name="KOTOR2" /> Surik attempted to intimidate Saquesh to release Adana, but Saquesh was not swayed and immediately attacked Surik. The former Jedi Knight [[Death/Legends|slew]] the Quarren<ref name="CSWE" /> and his [[Security guard/Legends|guards]] and released Adana from her [[Prison/Legends|cell]]. Surik returned to Nadaa, who thanked her profusely for her help.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Thank you! But… won't he track us down and kidnap her again?|Nadaa, after Surik agrees to free Adana|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
Nadaa loved her daughter Adana immensely and was desperate to speak to one of Saquesh's underlings to convince them to release her. When Surik agreed to help Nadaa, she was wary of the prospect of Saquesh finding her daughter again. She was exceedingly grateful when Surik was able to return Adana to her. Nadaa had [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and blonde [[Hair/Legends|hair]] kept in a ponytail.<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Equipment==
Nadaa wore tan-colored [[Robe/Legends|robes]] with a brown [[Clothing/Legends|undershirt]] and a pair of [[Boot/Legends|boots]].<ref name="KOTOR2" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Yes… I suppose that is one way we can stay together. Oh, this is terrible. This is terrible! But I must be with Adana.|Nadaa, to Surik if the player convinces her to sell herself to Saquesh|<ref name="KOTOR2" />}}
Nadaa first appeared in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. The player can convince Nadaa to sell herself to Saquesh to be with her daughter, which results in [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] points. It is also possible for Adana to be sold into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] if the player tells Saquesh to sell her quickly, which also results in dark side points. If this path is followed, the refugee leader [[Hussef]] will later note that Nadaa went mad and vanished.<ref name="KOTOR2" /> The [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' states that Surik did help Nadaa while on Nar Shaddaa, confirming the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] option in the game as [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1st}}
